question.text: An exporter hedges EUR 500,000 receivable by buying 500 lots of EURINR put at strike 91.00, premium Rs. 0.30. At expiry, EURINR is 92.50. What is the effective realization?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Put is OTM (spot 92.50 > strike 91.00), so not exercised. Exporter sells at spot 92.50, minus premium = 92.50 - 0.30 = Rs. 92.20. Option hedge allows upside participation. **The Trap:** Rs. 91.00 assumes put is exercised (wrong — not optimal when spot > strike). Rs. 90.70 is Strike - Premium (only applies when put IS exercised). **Cross-Exam Context:** Options vs futures hedging comparison is key Series I topic. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This shows the advantage of options: exporter captured 1.20 more than the futures locked rate would have been (approx 91.00 area), minus premium.
